summary: VFDP-16 is a governance proposal to establish a dual-entity structure for VITA-FAST, transitioning from an IP-NFT to a real-world asset structure. It proposes creating a Wyoming DUNA for community governance and a Delaware LLC for operations, treasury management, IP sublicensing, and equity financing to commercialize autophagy research from the Korolchuk Lab at Newcastle University.

content_blocks.title: One-Liner and Teaser
content_blocks.content: Proposal to establish dual-entity structure for VITA-FAST: (1) VitaFast DUNA (Wyoming Decentralized Unincorporated Nonprofit Association) for community governance and token holder membership, and (2) VitaFast LLC for business operations. Implements VitaDAO's Coin-to-Company (C2C) model. DUNA provides liability protection for VITA-FAST token holders without requiring identity verification for governance participation. LLC holds treasury funds, sublicenses research IP, and enables equity financing for Korolchuk Lab's autophagy research commercialization. DUNA will contract with third party as ministerial agent for limited operational functions.

content_blocks.title: Entity 1: VitaFast DUNA (Community Layer)
content_blocks.content: Wyoming Decentralized Unincorporated Nonprofit Association. Provides liability protection for VITA-FAST token holders. Membership conferred by Charter and Association Agreement upon holding VitaFast token. Coordinates governance and community decision-making. Does NOT hold treasury (avoids securities classification). Oversees Ministerial Agent's activities.

content_blocks.title: Entity 2: VitaFast LLC (Operating Layer)
content_blocks.content: Holds and manages VITA-FAST treasury funds. Conducts all business operations, sublicenses research IP, manages commercialization. Enables equity issuance through compliant securities exemptions (Reg D/S/CF/A). VitaDAO (through Vitality Now) exerts influence as majority voting shareholder in LLC, with shares granted conditional on KYC/AML based on locked token holdings. Implements categorical separation: tokens are utility instruments for community membership, equity flows through LLC via established exemptions.

content_blocks.title: Motivation - The Challenge and C2C Solution
content_blocks.content: Challenge: token-based projects face tension between enabling broad community participation without triggering securities compliance while creating value realization pathways. C2C Solution: categorical separation of Tokens (utility instruments for community membership/governance) and Equity (securities issued separately through compliant exemptions). Same person can be both DAO member AND shareholder through distinct pathways.

content_blocks.title: Regulatory Context
content_blocks.content: Wyoming enacted DUNA Act (effective July 1, 2024) providing legal recognition for DAOs. Combined with Delaware LLC framework, enables dual-entity structure providing: legal existence and liability protection, entity-level taxation (avoiding pass-through complexity), permits for-profit activities while maintaining nonprofit governance layer, positions tokens to avoid securities classification.

content_blocks.title: Commercial Imperative
content_blocks.content: Current market dynamics favor equity-based financing in U.S. for strategic capital in biomedical research. Dual-entity structure positions VITA-FAST to pursue equity financing from strategic investors, pharmaceutical companies, and longevity-focused funds while maintaining community governance.

content_blocks.title: VitaFast DUNA Specifications
content_blocks.content: Purpose: community coordination layer with legal recognition and liability protection for token holders. Key characteristics: Liability shield for members from personal liability; Membership conferred by Charter and Association Agreement upon holding VITA-FAST governance token (no separate application required); Governance rights via Snapshot voting; No treasury held (avoids securities classification); Ministerial Agent agreement for ministerial services (appointing counsel, disbursing approved funds, record-keeping, reporting) under DUNA oversight. Responsibilities: coordinate governance processes, provide formal membership structure, execute governance decisions not requiring treasury operations, maintain alignment between community governance and LLC operations, report to token holders on governance matters.

content_blocks.title: VitaFast LLC Specifications
content_blocks.content: Purpose: operating company conducting all business activities, holding license to IP and treasury, generating revenue, enabling equity issuance through compliant securities exemptions.

content_blocks.title: LLC Key Characteristics and Responsibilities
content_blocks.content: Key characteristics: All commercial activity flows through LLC; Holds and manages VITA-FAST funds (from IP-NFT fractionalization and token sales); Perpetual, worldwide, sublicensable license to develop and commercialize research IP from Newcastle autophagy program, with full ownership of developed products; Can issue equity via Reg D/S/CF/A + Rule 701 compliant pathways; QSBS eligible for potential tax benefits. Responsibilities: Execute VITA-FAST governance decisions on treasury; Sublicense research IP to development partners under perpetual worldwide license from VitaDAO; Enter contracts with Newcastle University, Korolchuk Lab, and partners; Execute sponsored research agreement addendums; Pursue equity financing from strategic investors; Handle regulatory compliance, tax obligations, reporting; Manage day-to-day operations for research advancement and commercialization.

content_blocks.title: Management Structure
content_blocks.content: Neuro Longevity LLC, managed by Oliver Armitage (core lead for VITA-FAST project), will be designated as sole manager of VitaFast LLC. Structure preserves token-holder governance through DUNA while ensuring LLC operates under clear traditional manager control for legal, tax, and commercial purposes.

content_blocks.title: Relationship Between DUNA and LLC - Categorical Separation
content_blocks.content: DUNA provides: blockchain-based governance coordination, liability protection, token holder membership and participation with possible non-profit interest perks like data access, NO treasury. Members required to apply to become shareholder in LLC. LLC provides: treasury management, business operations, sublicenses research IP, equity issuance, all commercial activity. Categorical separation ensures: DUNA = Membership (token holders are DAO members with governance voice but no automatic equity rights); LLC = Ownership (shareholders hold equity with economic rights via separate agreements); Tokens ≠ Securities (utility/commodity classification maintained); Equity = Securities (issued only through compliant exemptions).

content_blocks.title: Treasury Transfer to LLC
content_blocks.content: Transfer VITA-FAST treasury funds to VitaFast LLC. Funds derived from IP-NFT fractionalization and token sales. LLC treasury enables traditional banking relationships for operational needs. On-chain transparency maintained through regular reporting. Funds remain dedicated to VITA-FAST purposes per Whitepaper and Assignment Agreements.

content_blocks.title: Equity Financing Pathways
content_blocks.content: LLC structure enables compliant equity financing through two pathways: (1) Community Member Route: Token holder → Voluntary token lock → KYC/AML → Apply for contributor equity → AIPP Agreement. (2) Investor Route: Investor → Capital investment (Reg D/S/CF/A) → KYC/AML & accreditation → Stock Purchase Agreement with token perk → Equity. Token Locking as Identity Infrastructure: voluntary token locking gains eligibility to apply for equity but creates no automatic right, no option to purchase, no enforceable claim. Enables cryptographic identity for shareholder voting and dividend distributions, commitment signaling demonstrating alignment, eligibility to petition for equity through compliant pathways.

content_blocks.title: Implementation Phase 1: Entity Formation
content_blocks.content: Phase 1 (now): File VitaFast DUNA formation documents with Wyoming Secretary of State; Establish DUNA governing principles incorporating VITA-FAST governance mechanisms; Form VitaFast LLC in Delaware; Register both entities with registered agent services; Designate initial DUNA administrators.

content_blocks.title: Implementation Phase 2 and 3
content_blocks.content: Phase 2 (30-60 days post-formation): Establish LLC treasury account; Execute treasury transfer from multisig to LLC; Align DUNA governance processes with VITA-FAST Snapshot; Establish reporting mechanisms between DUNA, LLC, and token holders; Update multisig configuration as needed. Phase 3 (Ongoing Operations): DUNA coordinates governance; LLC executes treasury operations; Regular updates to VITA-FAST community; Annual compliance and financial reporting; Governance proposals for major decisions per existing framework. Execution: Upon Phase 2 Snapshot approval, current multisig signers coordinate entity formation and treasury transfer with confirmation reported in Discord and VITA-FAST governance channels.

content_blocks.title: Benefits
content_blocks.content: Regulatory Clarity: tokens stay utility; equity flows through established exemptions. Broad Participation: community members don't need accreditation to hold tokens. Institutional Ready: traditional investors can use familiar equity structures via LLC. On-Chain Execution: voting and distributions via locked tokens as identity. Liability Protection: both DUNA members and LLC shareholders have liability shields. Tax Efficiency: entity-level taxation; potential QSBS benefits for individual and passthrough LLC shareholders. Decentralization Preserved: token holder governance remains supreme via DUNA coordination. Commercialization Enabled: LLC provides clear counterparty for pharma partnerships and sublicenses research IP.

content_blocks.title: Risks and Mitigations
content_blocks.content: Risk: DUNA framework untested | Impact: Medium | Mitigation: DUNA modeled on established UNA law; Wyoming has strong DAO track record; a16z backing. Risk: Dual-entity complexity | Impact: Low | Mitigation: Clear separation of responsibilities. Risk: Centralization concerns | Impact: Low | Mitigation: DUNA designed for decentralized orgs; LLC executes governance decisions, doesn't make them. Risk: Regulatory changes | Impact: Medium | Mitigation: Current U.S. administration favorable; dual-entity provides better protection than no entity.

content_blocks.title: Alignment with Existing Framework
content_blocks.content: Proposal consistent with: VDP-111 (VITA-FAST Governance): token holder authority preserved via DUNA; LLC executes treasury decisions. C2C Model: implements categorical separation of tokens (utility) and equity (securities). VITA-FAST Whitepaper: enables commercialization pathway for autophagy research IP. Assignment Agreements: LLC manages sublicensing rights per Newcastle University agreements. FAM Membership Agreement: token holder rights and governance participation maintained via DUNA.

key_findings: Dual-entity structure proposed: VitaFast DUNA (Wyoming) for community governance and VitaFast LLC (Delaware) for business operations
Purpose is to enable equity financing for commercialization of Korolchuk Lab's autophagy research from Newcastle University while maintaining decentralized token holder governance
LLC holds perpetual, worldwide, sublicensable license to develop and commercialize research IP from Newcastle autophagy program with full ownership of developed products
VitaDAO (through Vitality Now) will be majority voting shareholder in LLC based on locked token holdings with KYC/AML
Neuro Longevity LLC (managed by Oliver Armitage, VITA-FAST core lead) designated as sole manager of VitaFast LLC
Treasury derived from IP-NFT fractionalization and token sales will transfer to LLC for operational management
Equity financing pathways through Reg D/S/CF/A exemptions for both community members (via voluntary token lock, KYC, application) and traditional investors
Implementation in 3 phases: entity formation (now), operational integration (30-60 days), ongoing operations
Key risk: DUNA framework untested (mitigated by Wyoming DAO track record and a16z backing)
Aligns with existing VDP-111 governance framework and enables commercialization while preserving decentralized governance
